dependencies.relationship: Run() consumes apikit.TokenPrefix and LoadConfig()-derived ConfigDir from 01_server_core
Run() and ShouldAutoPromote() use *sql.DB from db.Open() and admin_config/users tables from 02_database_layer

task_groups.title: Write Tests: First Boot Detection and First Boot Sequence (Part 1)

subtasks.title: Write test file skeleton and helper utilities for bootstrap tests
details: Create internal/bootstrap/bootstrap_test.go with package bootstrap_test.
Implement openMemoryDB() helper that calls db.OpenMemory and applies schema (users, admin_config tables).
Implement insertUser(db *sql.DB) helper for seeding test users.
Implement setAdminConfig(db *sql.DB, key, value string) and queryAdminConfig(db *sql.DB, key string) (string, error) helpers.
Implement hexSHA256(s string) string helper computing hex-encoded SHA-256.
Implement newTestLogger() returning a *logrus.Logger with a logrus test hook for capturing entries.
Implement fileExists(path string) bool and readFileBytes(path string) []byte helpers.

subtasks.title: Write tests for first boot detection (REQ-1)
details: Write TestRun_FirstBoot_UserCountQueried (TS-04-1): zero-user DB with valid AdminEmail; assert Run returns nil and admin_email is stored.
Write TestRun_FirstBoot_Classification (TS-04-2): zero users → first boot sequence executes, admin_email stored.
Write TestRun_SubsequentBoot_Classification (TS-04-3): insert user, set matching ADMIN_TOKEN and admin_token_hash, assert Run returns nil without re-writing admin_email.
Write TestRun_BrokenDB_UserCountFails (TS-04-E1): pass a closed/broken *sql.DB; assert Run returns non-nil error.

subtasks.title: Write tests for first boot sequence – email requirement and storage (REQ-2.1, REQ-2.2)
details: Write TestRun_FirstBoot_NoEmail (TS-04-4): zero-user DB, AdminEmail=''; assert non-nil error containing 'admin-email'.
Write TestRun_FirstBoot_EmailStored (TS-04-5): zero-user DB, AdminEmail='admin@example.com'; assert admin_config.admin_email == 'admin@example.com' after Run.

subtasks.title: Write tests for first boot sequence – token generation, hashing, and file writing (REQ-2.3 – REQ-2.8)
details: Write TestRun_FirstBoot_TokenFormat (TS-04-6): verify token file content matches '^ak_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$'.
Write TestRun_FirstBoot_TokenHashStored (TS-04-7): verify admin_token_hash == hexSHA256(token_file_content) and is 64 lowercase hex chars.
Write TestRun_FirstBoot_TokenFilePermissions (TS-04-8): skip on Windows; verify token file mode == 0600.
Write TestRun_FirstBoot_WarnLog (TS-04-9): capture logrus entries; assert warn-level entry contains absolute path of admin_token file.
Write TestRun_FirstBoot_ReturnsNil (TS-04-10): valid params → Run returns nil.
Write TestRun_FirstBoot_NoTrailingNewline (TS-04-11): readFileBytes; assert last byte != 0x0A.

subtasks.title: Write tests for first boot error paths (REQ-2.E1 – REQ-2.E4)
details: Write TestRun_FirstBoot_RandFailure (TS-04-E2): inject broken rand reader via package-level var or dependency injection; assert error returned, no token file written, no hash stored.
Write TestRun_FirstBoot_FileWriteFailure (TS-04-E3): use read-only tmpDir; assert non-nil error wrapping OS error.
Write TestRun_FirstBoot_DBWriteFailure (TS-04-E4): drop admin_config table before Run; assert non-nil error.
Write TestRun_NeverCallsOsExit (TS-04-E5, TS-04-E12): empty AdminEmail triggers error; assert test process continues; grep source for 'os.Exit' and 'log.Fatal' and assert absent.

checks: All test functions in bootstrap_test.go compile without errors: go build ./internal/bootstrap/...
Test file exists at internal/bootstrap/bootstrap_test.go with all named test functions present.
go vet ./internal/bootstrap/... reports no issues.
Helper utilities (openMemoryDB, insertUser, setAdminConfig, queryAdminConfig, hexSHA256, newTestLogger) are defined and used consistently across test cases.

task_groups.title: Write Tests: Subsequent Boot Sequence and Token Rotation

subtasks.title: Write tests for subsequent boot – file-presence guard and env var validation (REQ-4.1 – REQ-4.3)
details: Write TestRun_SubsequentBoot_FileExists (TS-04-17): insert user, write admin_token file to ConfigDir; assert error containing 'admin_token file exists'.
Write TestRun_SubsequentBoot_NoEnvVar (TS-04-18): insert user, no token file, unset ADMIN_TOKEN; assert error containing 'ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable is required'.
Write TestRun_SubsequentBoot_HashComparison (TS-04-19): insert user, correct token in env, matching hash in admin_config; assert Run returns nil (proves SHA-256 comparison path runs).

subtasks.title: Write tests for subsequent boot – hash mismatch, email ignore, success, missing hash (REQ-4.4 – REQ-4.7)
details: Write TestRun_SubsequentBoot_WrongToken (TS-04-20): wrong ADMIN_TOKEN; assert error containing 'does not match'.
Write TestRun_SubsequentBoot_IgnoresAdminEmail (TS-04-21): non-empty AdminEmail on subsequent boot; assert Run returns nil, admin_email in admin_config unchanged.
Write TestRun_SubsequentBoot_Success (TS-04-22): correct token, file absent; assert nil return.
Write TestRun_SubsequentBoot_NoStoredHash (TS-04-23): no admin_token_hash in admin_config; assert error containing 'no admin token hash found'.

subtasks.title: Write tests for subsequent boot error paths (REQ-4.E1, REQ-4.E2)
details: Write TestRun_SubsequentBoot_DBReadFailure (TS-04-E7): insert user, no token file, ADMIN_TOKEN set, but drop admin_config table; assert non-nil error.

Write TestConstantTimeCompare_UsedInSource (TS-04-E8): read internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go source; assert contains 'subtle.ConstantTimeCompare' and '"crypto/subtle"'. Also write functional sub-test: same hash compares equal, different hashes compare unequal.

subtasks.title: Write tests for token rotation happy path (REQ-5.1 – REQ-5.5)
details: Write TestRun_ResetToken_GeneratesToken (TS-04-24): insert user, ResetToken=true; assert token file contains valid format, Run returns nil.
Write TestRun_ResetToken_InvalidatesOldHash (TS-04-25): store old hash, run rotation; assert new stored hash != old hash and equals hexSHA256(new token file content).
Write TestRun_ResetToken_FilePermissions (TS-04-26): skip on Windows; assert token file mode == 0600 after rotation.
Write TestRun_ResetToken_WarnLog (TS-04-27): capture log entries; assert warn-level entry contains absolute path of admin_token file.
Write TestRun_ResetToken_SkipsGuards (TS-04-28): ResetToken=true, ADMIN_TOKEN unset, no prior token file; assert nil returned (proves file-presence and env var guards are skipped).

subtasks.title: Write tests for token rotation next-restart behavior and error paths (REQ-5.6, REQ-5.E1 – REQ-5.E3)
details: Write TestRun_ResetToken_NextRestart_FileGuardFires (TS-04-29): simulate post-rotation restart (token file left, ResetToken=false, users exist); assert error containing 'admin_token file exists'.
Write TestRun_ResetToken_RandFailure (TS-04-E9): inject broken rand, ResetToken=true; assert error returned, old hash in admin_config unchanged.
Write TestRun_ResetToken_FileWriteFailure (TS-04-E10): read-only ConfigDir, ResetToken=true; assert non-nil error wrapping OS error.
Write TestRun_ResetToken_DBWriteFailure (TS-04-E11): drop admin_config table before rotation; assert non-nil error.

checks: All subsequent boot and token rotation test functions compile: go build ./internal/bootstrap/...
go vet ./internal/bootstrap/... reports no issues.
Test function names match exactly those referenced in TS-04-17 through TS-04-29 and TS-04-E7 through TS-04-E11.
t.Skip is used appropriately on file-permission tests when runtime.GOOS == 'windows'.

task_groups.title: Write Tests: ShouldAutoPromote, Token Format/Hashing, Execution Order, File Path Resolution

subtasks.title: Write tests for ShouldAutoPromote (REQ-3)
details: Write TestShouldAutoPromote_Match (TS-04-12): store admin_email, call with matching email; assert (true, nil).
Write TestShouldAutoPromote_NoMatch (TS-04-13): store admin_email, call with different email; assert (false, nil).
Write TestShouldAutoPromote_NoConfig (TS-04-14): empty admin_config; assert (false, nil).
Write TestShouldAutoPromote_NewUserConvention (TS-04-15): call with matching email; assert (true, nil); add comment documenting caller responsibility for new-user gate.
Write TestShouldAutoPromote_InfoLog (TS-04-16): simulate OAuth callback calling ShouldAutoPromote then logging at info; assert info-level log entry contains email.
Write TestShouldAutoPromote_DBError (TS-04-E6): broken DB; assert (false, non-nil error).

subtasks.title: Write tests for token format and hashing properties (REQ-6)
details: Write TestGenerateToken_Format (TS-04-30): call internal generateToken helper (or via Run + file read); assert regex '^ak_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$' and hex portion exactly 64 chars.
Write TestHashToken_KnownValue (TS-04-31): hash a fixed token string; assert 64-char lowercase hex equal to expected SHA-256.
Write TestConstantTimeCompare_Correctness (TS-04-32): compareTokenHashes(h, h)==true, compareTokenHashes(h, differentH)==false.
Write TestGenerateToken_NoCryptoRand (TS-04-33): read source file; assert no 'math/rand' import, assert 'crypto/rand' import present.
Write TestTokenFile_NoTrailingNewline (TS-04-34): first boot; readFileBytes; assert last byte != 0x0A and content matches token regex.

subtasks.title: Write tests for bootstrap execution order and process termination contract (REQ-7)
details: Write TestRun_SchemaExistsBeforeRun (TS-04-35): openMemoryDB (schema applied), assert users and admin_config tables exist before calling Run; Run returns nil.
Write TestRun_ResetToken_Priority (TS-04-36): ResetToken=true with empty AdminEmail and zero users; assert Run returns nil (rotation bypasses first-boot AdminEmail check).
Write TestRun_ErrorIsNonFatal (TS-04-37): trigger error (empty AdminEmail, zero users); assert Run returns non-nil error and test process continues (no os.Exit).
Write TestRun_SingleInstanceDocumented (TS-04-38): read source; assert 'Concurrent' or 'single-instance' appears in Run godoc; also run sequential calls without deadlock.
Write TestRun_NoOsExitInSource (TS-04-E12): read bootstrap.go source; assert 'os.Exit' and 'log.Fatal' absent.

subtasks.title: Write tests for token file path resolution (REQ-8)
details: Write TestTokenFilePath_XDGSet (TS-04-39): set XDG_CONFIG_HOME to a tmpDir, ConfigDir = tmpDir+'/apikit'; Run with zero users; assert file at ConfigDir/admin_token.
Write TestTokenFilePath_XDGUnset (TS-04-40): unset XDG_CONFIG_HOME, ConfigDir = another tmpDir; Run with zero users; assert file at ConfigDir/admin_token.
Write TestTokenFilePath_UsesConfigDir (TS-04-41): supply explicit ConfigDir; assert file appears at exactly ConfigDir+'/admin_token'.
Write TestTokenFilePath_NonExistentDir (TS-04-E13): set ConfigDir to a path that cannot be created (e.g., inside /proc on Linux or similar); assert non-nil error, file not created.

subtasks.title: Write property and smoke test stubs
details: Write property tests using testing/quick or table-driven approach: TestProp_TokenUniqueness (TS-04-P1), TestProp_HashDeterminism (TS-04-P2), TestProp_HashDiscrimination (TS-04-P3).
Write TestProp_FilePresentGuardBlocks (TS-04-P4): for any subsequent boot with file present, Run returns non-nil error.
Write TestProp_OldTokenInvalidatedAfterRotation (TS-04-P5): rotate, assert old hash no longer matches.
Write TestProp_FirstBootRequiresEmail (TS-04-P6), TestProp_ShouldAutoPromoteNoConfig (TS-04-P7), TestProp_RunNeverTerminates (TS-04-P8).

Write smoke tests as integration test functions: TestSmoke_FirstBoot (TS-04-SMOKE-1), TestSmoke_SubsequentBoot (TS-04-SMOKE-2), TestSmoke_FilePresentGuard (TS-04-SMOKE-3), TestSmoke_TokenRotation (TS-04-SMOKE-4), TestSmoke_AutoPromotion (TS-04-SMOKE-5), TestSmoke_EndToEnd (TS-04-SMOKE-6).

checks: All test functions in groups 1–3 compile without error: go build ./internal/bootstrap/...
go vet ./internal/bootstrap/... reports no issues.
go test ./internal/bootstrap/... -count=1 -list '.*' shows all expected test function names.
No test function body is empty (each has at least one assertion or t.Skip call).

task_groups.title: Implement Package Skeleton and Core Token Primitives

subtasks.title: Create internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go with package declaration and public types
details: Create internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go with package bootstrap.
Declare BootstrapParams struct with all fields: DB *sql.DB, AdminEmail string, ResetToken bool, ConfigDir string, TokenPrefix string, Logger *logrus.Logger.
Add func Run(ctx context.Context, params BootstrapParams) error stub with panic("not implemented").
Add func ShouldAutoPromote(ctx context.Context, sqlDB *sql.DB, email string) (bool, error) stub with panic("not implemented").
Import required stdlib packages: context, crypto/rand, crypto/sha256, crypto/subtle, database/sql, encoding/hex, errors, fmt, os, path/filepath.
Import github.com/sirupsen/logrus.
Ensure the godoc comment on Run mentions concurrent calls not supported and single-instance startup requirement (satisfies TS-04-38).

subtasks.title: Implement generateToken(prefix string) (string, error) internal helper
details: Implement generateToken: allocate [32]byte, call crypto/rand.Read, hex-encode to 64 lowercase hex chars using encoding/hex.EncodeToString, prepend prefix+'_admin_'.
Return (token string, error); wrap crypto/rand error with fmt.Errorf.
Never import or reference math/rand.

subtasks.title: Implement hashToken(token string) string and compareTokenHashes(a, b string) bool internal helpers
details: Implement hashToken: compute sha256.Sum256([]byte(token)), hex-encode with encoding/hex.EncodeToString, return 64-char lowercase hex string.
Implement compareTokenHashes(a, b string) bool: use subtle.ConstantTimeCompare([]byte(a), []byte(b)) == 1.
Import crypto/subtle explicitly.

subtasks.title: Implement tokenFilePath(configDir string) string internal helper
details: Implement tokenFilePath(configDir string) string: return filepath.Join(configDir, "admin_token").
Note: path resolution (XDG vs CWD) is handled by the caller passing ConfigDir via BootstrapParams; this helper only joins the filename. Document this in a comment.

subtasks.title: Implement writeTokenFile(path, token string) error internal helper
details: Implement writeTokenFile: call os.WriteFile(path, []byte(token), 0600).
Do NOT append a trailing newline to the token bytes.
Wrap any error with fmt.Errorf.

checks: go build ./internal/bootstrap/... succeeds (package compiles).
go vet ./internal/bootstrap/... reports no issues.
grep -r 'math/rand' internal/bootstrap/ returns no matches.
grep 'crypto/subtle' internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go returns a match.
grep 'Concurrent\|single-instance' internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go returns a match in the Run godoc.
Tests for token primitives pass: go test ./internal/bootstrap/... -run 'TestGenerateToken|TestHashToken|TestConstantTime|TestTokenFilePath' -count=1 -v

task_groups.title: Implement First Boot Sequence in Run()

subtasks.title: Implement user count query and boot type detection in Run()
details: Remove panic stub from Run().
First action in Run: if params.ResetToken is true, jump directly to rotation (call runTokenRotation, return result).
Otherwise query: var count int; err = params.DB.QueryRowContext(ctx, 'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users').Scan(&count); wrap and return any error.
If count == 0 call runFirstBoot(ctx, params); else call runSubsequentBoot(ctx, params).
Return the result of whichever branch was called.

subtasks.title: Implement runFirstBoot() – admin email validation and storage
details: Implement runFirstBoot(ctx context.Context, params BootstrapParams) error.
If params.AdminEmail == '' return errors.New("first boot: --admin-email is required").
Execute INSERT OR REPLACE INTO admin_config (key, value) VALUES ('admin_email', ?) with params.AdminEmail; wrap any DB error.

subtasks.title: Implement runFirstBoot() – token generation, hashing, file writing, and logging
details: Call generateToken(params.TokenPrefix); wrap and return any error.
Call hashToken(token); execute INSERT OR REPLACE INTO admin_config (key, value) VALUES ('admin_token_hash', ?) with the hash; wrap DB error.
Compute filePath = tokenFilePath(params.ConfigDir); call writeTokenFile(filePath, token); wrap and return any OS error.

Log: params.Logger.Warnf("admin token written to %s — save this token securely and delete the file before restarting", absPath) where absPath = filepath.Abs(filePath) (ignore Abs error, fall back to filePath).
Return nil.

subtasks.title: Ensure runFirstBoot() does not call os.Exit or log.Fatal
details: Code review: verify runFirstBoot and all helpers it calls contain no os.Exit, os.Exit(), log.Fatal, or logrus.Fatal calls.
All error conditions return non-nil error values.
Add a build-time check comment: // Run and its callees must never call os.Exit or log.Fatal.

checks: go build ./internal/bootstrap/... succeeds.
go vet ./internal/bootstrap/... reports no issues.
First boot tests pass: go test ./internal/bootstrap/... -run 'TestRun_FirstBoot|TestRun_BrokenDB' -count=1 -v
grep -n 'os.Exit\|log.Fatal' internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go returns no matches.
04-REQ-2.1 acceptance criteria met: empty AdminEmail on first boot returns error containing 'admin-email'.
04-REQ-2.5 acceptance criteria met: token file created with mode 0600.

task_groups.title: Implement Subsequent Boot Sequence and Token Rotation in Run()

subtasks.title: Implement runSubsequentBoot() – file-presence guard and ADMIN_TOKEN env var check
details: Implement runSubsequentBoot(ctx context.Context, params BootstrapParams) error.
Compute filePath = tokenFilePath(params.ConfigDir).
Call os.Stat(filePath); if the file exists (err == nil), return fmt.Errorf("admin_token file exists at %s: save the token securely and delete the file before restarting", filePath).
Read envToken = os.Getenv("ADMIN_TOKEN"); if empty, return errors.New("ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable is required").
AdminEmail field is silently ignored (no logging, no DB write).

subtasks.title: Implement runSubsequentBoot() – hash retrieval and constant-time comparison
details: Query stored hash: var storedHash string; err = params.DB.QueryRowContext(ctx, 'SELECT value FROM admin_config WHERE key = "admin_token_hash"').Scan(&storedHash).
If err == sql.ErrNoRows return errors.New("no admin token hash found in database; run with --reset-admin-token to generate a new token").
Wrap any other DB error with fmt.Errorf.
Compute candidateHash = hashToken(envToken).
If !compareTokenHashes(candidateHash, storedHash) return errors.New("ADMIN_TOKEN does not match the stored admin token").
Return nil.

subtasks.title: Implement runTokenRotation() – token generation, storage, file writing, and logging
details: Implement runTokenRotation(ctx context.Context, params BootstrapParams) error.
Call generateToken(params.TokenPrefix); wrap and return any error (old hash must NOT be overwritten if rand fails).
Call hashToken(token); execute INSERT OR REPLACE INTO admin_config (key, value) VALUES ('admin_token_hash', ?); wrap DB error.
Compute filePath = tokenFilePath(params.ConfigDir); call writeTokenFile(filePath, token); wrap OS error.
Log absolute path at warn level (same as first boot logging).
Return nil on success.
Skips file-presence guard and ADMIN_TOKEN check — these are not called in runTokenRotation.

subtasks.title: Implement ShouldAutoPromote()
details: Remove panic stub from ShouldAutoPromote.
Query: var adminEmail string; err = sqlDB.QueryRowContext(ctx, 'SELECT value FROM admin_config WHERE key = "admin_email"').Scan(&adminEmail).
If err == sql.ErrNoRows return (false, nil).
If err != nil return (false, fmt.Errorf("ShouldAutoPromote: %w", err)).
Return (adminEmail == email, nil).
Note in godoc: this function is intended for new user creation only; callers must not invoke it on existing user updates.

checks: go build ./internal/bootstrap/... succeeds.
go vet ./internal/bootstrap/... reports no issues.
Subsequent boot tests pass: go test ./internal/bootstrap/... -run 'TestRun_SubsequentBoot|TestRun_ResetToken|TestShouldAutoPromote' -count=1 -v
04-REQ-4.1 acceptance criteria met: error message matches 'admin_token file exists at %s'.
04-REQ-4.7 acceptance criteria met: error message contains 'no admin token hash found'.
04-REQ-5.5 acceptance criteria met: rotation returns nil when ADMIN_TOKEN is unset.
grep 'subtle.ConstantTimeCompare' internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go returns a match.

task_groups.title: Wiring Verification: End-to-End Integration and Execution Path Validation

subtasks.title: Trace execution paths and verify entry point call chains (PATH-1 through PATH-6)
details: Verify PATH-1 (first boot): Run is called after DB open, stores admin_email, generates token, writes file, logs warn, returns nil — trace through runFirstBoot implementation.
Verify PATH-2 (subsequent boot valid): Run calls runSubsequentBoot which calls os.Stat, os.Getenv, DB query, compareTokenHashes; returns nil on success.
Verify PATH-3 (file-presence guard): runSubsequentBoot returns non-nil error when file exists; caller (main) would call log.Fatal.
Verify PATH-4 (token rotation): Run detects ResetToken=true, calls runTokenRotation, skips all other checks, returns nil.
Verify PATH-5 (auto-promotion): ShouldAutoPromote queries admin_config, returns (true, nil) on match; caller logs info.
Verify PATH-6 (end-to-end): run TestSmoke_EndToEnd and confirm all three phases complete without error.

subtasks.title: Verify return value propagation from Run() to server binary main()
details: Confirm Run() never calls os.Exit or log.Fatal — errors bubble up as return values.
Inspect any call site of Run in main (or cmd) package: verify pattern is 'if err := bootstrap.Run(ctx, params); err != nil { log.Fatal(err) }'.
Verify ShouldAutoPromote return values are consumed by the OAuth callback caller and not silently discarded.

subtasks.title: Run all smoke tests with real components and verify no stubs remain
details: Run: go test ./internal/bootstrap/... -run 'TestSmoke_' -count=1 -v and verify all 6 smoke tests pass.
Audit for stub markers: grep -rn 'panic("not implemented")\|// TODO\|// FIXME' internal/bootstrap/ — resolve any found.
Confirm no bare 'return nil' exists in non-trivial functions (Run, runFirstBoot, runSubsequentBoot, runTokenRotation, ShouldAutoPromote) without preceding logic.
Run: go test ./... -count=1 to confirm no regressions in dependent packages.

subtasks.title: Verify cross-spec entry points and interface contracts
details: Confirm BootstrapParams.DB accepts *sql.DB as provided by 02_database_layer db.Open() (type compatibility check via go build).
Confirm BootstrapParams.TokenPrefix is populated from apikit.TokenPrefix (01_server_core) — verify the call site in main or cmd package.
Confirm BootstrapParams.ConfigDir is populated from LoadConfig() result (01_server_core) — inspect how ConfigDir is derived and passed.
Confirm ShouldAutoPromote signature (ctx context.Context, sqlDB *sql.DB, email string) (bool, error) matches what the OAuth spec will call — document the interface contract in the package godoc.
Verify the admin_config table schema (key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT) matches what bootstrap reads/writes — cross-check against 02_database_layer schema definition.
